text:
bring before the Court cogent material to indicate that he had the 
financial capacity and had actually advanced the amount in question 
by way of loan. In the case at hand, the appellant had categorically 
stated in his deposition and reiterated in the cross-examination that 
he had withdrawn the amount from the bank in Faizabad (Typed 
Copy of his deposition in the paperbook wrongly mentions this as 
‘Firozabad’). The Court ought not to have summarily rejected such 
stand, more so when respondent no.2 did not make any serious 
attempt to dispel/negate such stand/statement of the appellant. 
Thus, on the one hand, the statement made before the Court, both 
in examination-in-chief and cross-examination, by the appellant with 
regard to withdrawing the money from the bank for giving it to the 
accused has been disbelieved whereas the argument on behalf of the 
accused that he had not received any payment of any loan amount
